title: Snapshot Context — ARIA Delta After Write Commands
overview: Extend action-context to optionally include ARIA snapshot deltas or full snapshots after write commands. Agents choose a context level per-request or per-session: 'state' (existing — URL/title/dialog), 'delta' (state + ARIA diff with refs for added elements), or 'full' (state + complete new snapshot with refs). Eliminates the snapshot→action→snapshot round-trip, saving ~500-2000 tokens and one HTTP call per interaction step.
scopeChallenge: Considered persistent/stable refs (fragile — DOM mutations silently invalidate nth-based locators), MutationObserver-based dirty tracking (too many false positives), and structural tree diffing with node matching (complex, EXPANSION territory). HOLD approach: reuse existing handleSnapshot + Diff.diffLines to produce a line-level delta with refs on added elements. The agent's last snapshot serves as the baseline — no extra ARIA capture before the write, no ref-overwrite risk.

prerequisites.assumption: action-context feature is landed (TASK-001–006 from action-context plan)
prerequisites.verification: src/action-context.ts exists with capturePageState, buildContextDelta, formatContextLine

nonGoals: Persistent/stable element refs across DOM mutations
Structural tree diffing with node matching or moved-element detection
Automatic context level selection (agent always chooses explicitly)
Per-tool-call context level in MCP (uses session-level setting)

contracts.shape: X-Browse-Context: state | delta | full (1 maps to state for backward compat)
prepareWriteContext(level, bm, buffers) → WriteContextCapture; finalizeWriteContext(capture, bm, buffers, result, command) → enriched result string
contracts.consistencyRule: For delta/full, finalizeWriteContext calls handleSnapshot which updates bm.refMap — refs are always from the post-write snapshot
Refs on added lines are valid Locators in the current refMap; returns empty string when snapshots are identical
Per-request header overrides session level; 'on' maps to 'state' for backward compat

failureModes.risk: handleSnapshot() called in finalizeWriteContext overwrites refMap — agent's pre-write refs become invalid mid-command
failureModes.mitigation: Snapshot runs AFTER the write command completes. The write command resolves refs from the existing refMap before finalizeWriteContext is called. New refs are for the agent's next action.

failureModes.risk: No baseline snapshot for delta (agent never ran snapshot before first write)
failureModes.mitigation: If bm.getLastSnapshot() returns null, fall back to full mode — take fresh snapshot after write, return entire output. First write gives full, subsequent writes give deltas.

failureModes.risk: Delta output is large when page changes significantly (e.g., SPA navigation disguised as same-URL)
failureModes.mitigation: If added+removed > 80% of total elements, prepend a note: '[snapshot-delta] major change — consider full snapshot'. Let agent decide.

failureModes.risk: handleSnapshot latency adds overhead to every write command in delta/full mode
failureModes.mitigation: handleSnapshot is ~10-30ms typical (ariaSnapshot ~5-20ms + parsing ~5-10ms). Acceptable for opt-in. State-only mode has zero snapshot overhead.

failureModes.risk: Snapshot options mismatch: baseline was -i -V but after snapshot uses -i only
failureModes.mitigation: Always use bm.getLastSnapshotOpts() for the after snapshot, matching the baseline's filter. Defaults to ['-i'] if no previous options.
Already bundled — imported in meta.ts for snapshot-diff. No new dependency.

testCoverageMap.codepath: formatAriaDelta() — ARIA line diff with ref preservation
finalizeWriteContext() — after-write enrichment at each level
server.ts context level parsing from X-Browse-Context header
Delta mode: click that adds/removes DOM elements → diff with refs
Full mode: click → full snapshot appended to result
State mode: backward compatible, no ARIA overhead
No baseline: first write in delta mode → falls back to full
Refs from delta are valid for subsequent commands
MCP write with delta context → ARIA diff in response
MCP read/meta commands → no context appended

tasks.title: Define ContextLevel type and update Session interface
tasks.description: Add the `ContextLevel` union type to `src/types.ts` alongside the existing action-context types. Add `contextLevel: ContextLevel` to the `Session` interface in `src/session-manager.ts` alongside the existing `contextEnabled` field (keep both — TASK-004 removes `contextEnabled` when it rewires server.ts). Update session creation in `getOrCreate()` to initialize `contextLevel: 'off'`. Add `WriteContextCapture` interface to types.ts for the before/after orchestration pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextLevel = 'off' | 'state' | 'delta' | 'full' exported from types.ts
Session interface has both contextEnabled (kept temporarily) and contextLevel fields
TypeScript compiles cleanly: tsc --noEmit succeeds

tasks.title: Implement ARIA delta formatter
tasks.description: Add `formatAriaDelta(baseline: string, current: string): string` to `src/action-context.ts`. This function takes two snapshot outputs (both with @refs), strips refs from both for comparison using `Diff.diffLines()`, then rebuilds the output with refs preserved on added lines from the 'current' text. Removed lines are shown without refs. Returns a summary line (`[snapshot-delta] +N -M =K`) followed by the diff lines, or empty string if identical. Import `diff` library (already in the bundle via meta.ts). Edge cases: empty baseline returns all lines as added; identical snapshots return empty string; large changes (>80% added+removed) prepend a note suggesting full snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: formatAriaDelta with identical inputs returns empty string
formatAriaDelta with added elements returns + lines with @refs from current text
formatAriaDelta with empty baseline returns full current text prefixed with + markers

tasks.title: Implement write context orchestrator (prepare/finalize)
tasks.description:
Add `prepareWriteContext(level: ContextLevel, bm: BrowserManager, buffers: SessionBuffers): Promise<WriteContextCapture>` and `finalizeWriteContext(capture: WriteContextCapture, bm: BrowserManager, buffers: SessionBuffers, result: string, command: string): Promise<string>` to `src/action-context.ts`. These replace the inline before/after logic in server.ts and mcp.ts.

`prepareWriteContext`: For 'state' — captures PageState only. For 'delta' — captures PageState + stores baseline text from `bm.getLastSnapshot()`. For 'full' — captures PageState. For 'off' — returns null capture.

`finalizeWriteContext`: For 'state' — existing behavior (capturePageState, buildContextDelta, formatContextLine). For 'delta' — run `handleSnapshot(bm.getLastSnapshotOpts() || ['-i'], bm)`, call `formatAriaDelta(baseline, newSnapshot)`, prepend state context line. For 'full' — run `handleSnapshot(opts, bm)`, prepend state context line, append full snapshot. For 'off' — return result unchanged.

Import `handleSnapshot` from `../snapshot`. Use `bm.getLastSnapshotOpts()` defaulting to `['-i']` for snapshot options. If delta mode has no baseline (getLastSnapshot returns null), fall back to full mode. `finalizeWriteContext` must wrap all snapshot/delta work in try/catch — context capture failures must never break the write command response (preserve the existing 'Don't let context capture failures break the command' pattern from server.ts and mcp.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: prepareWriteContext('off', ...) returns a capture that causes finalizeWriteContext to return result unchanged
finalizeWriteContext in delta mode calls handleSnapshot and formatAriaDelta, appending delta to result
Edge case: delta mode with no baseline (null getLastSnapshot) falls back to full mode

tasks.title: Wire context levels into server.ts command pipeline
tasks.description: Refactor `src/server.ts` to use context levels instead of boolean. Change `RequestOptions.contextEnabled: boolean` to `RequestOptions.contextLevel: ContextLevel`. Parse `X-Browse-Context` header: '1' or 'state' → 'state', 'delta' → 'delta', 'full' → 'full', absent → 'off'. Resolve effective level: per-request header overrides `session.contextLevel`. Replace the inline before/after capturePageState + buildContextDelta logic in the WRITE_COMMANDS branch with `prepareWriteContext()` before and `finalizeWriteContext()` after. Update the `set context` detection (line ~338) to read levels from result. Update profileSession creation to use `contextLevel: 'off'`. Remove the deprecated `contextEnabled` field from Session usage and `RequestOptions` (completing the migration started in TASK-001). Import ContextLevel from types.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: X-Browse-Context: delta header causes write commands to return ARIA diff with refs after the action result
X-Browse-Context: 1 header continues to work (backward compatible, state-only context)
Read and meta commands are not affected by any context level (zero overhead)
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert server.ts to use contextEnabled boolean

tasks.title: Wire context levels into MCP server
tasks.description: Refactor `src/mcp.ts` to use the shared `prepareWriteContext()` / `finalizeWriteContext()` functions instead of the inline capturePageState + buildContextDelta logic. MCP currently always runs state-level context for write commands. Add a module-level `mcpContextLevel: ContextLevel` variable (default: 'state') that can be changed via the `set context` command. When a write command runs: call `prepareWriteContext(mcpContextLevel, bm, buffers)` before, `finalizeWriteContext(capture, bm, buffers, result, command)` after. Detect `set context` in the write result to update `mcpContextLevel`.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP write commands append ARIA delta when mcpContextLevel is 'delta'
MCP read and meta commands have zero context overhead regardless of level
Edge case: set context full via MCP changes subsequent write responses to include full snapshot
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert mcp.ts to inline context logic

tasks.title: Update --context CLI flag to accept levels
tasks.description: Modify `src/cli.ts` context flag parsing (line ~653-660). Currently `--context` is boolean. Change to accept optional value: `--context` (no value) → 'state' (backward compat), `--context delta` → 'delta', `--context full` → 'full'. The value is the next arg if it matches state/delta/full, otherwise default to 'state'. Set `X-Browse-Context` header to the string value instead of '1'. Update `cliFlags.context` from boolean to string. Update help text to show: `--context [state|delta|full]  Enable action context (state=changes only, delta=ARIA diff, full=complete snapshot)`. Also support env var: `BROWSE_CONTEXT=delta`.
acceptanceCriteria: --context without value sets X-Browse-Context: state header (backward compat with existing --context behavior)
--context delta sets X-Browse-Context: delta header
Edge case: --context followed by a command name (not a level) defaults to state, does not consume the command as the level
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert cli.ts context flag to boolean

tasks.title: Extend set context command for levels
tasks.description: Update `src/commands/write.ts` case 'context' (line ~680-688) to accept: `set context off|on|state|delta|full`. 'on' maps to 'state' for backward compat. 'off' maps to 'off'. Return descriptive message for each level. Update the detection in `src/server.ts` (line ~338-340) to parse the level from the result string and set `session.contextLevel` accordingly. The current code checks `args[1]?.toLowerCase() === 'on'` — extend to parse all valid levels.
acceptanceCriteria: set context delta returns 'Context level set to delta — write commands will include ARIA snapshot diff'
set context on returns backward-compatible message and sets level to 'state'
Edge case: set context (no value) returns current context level
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert write.ts and server.ts set context changes

tasks.title: Integration tests for snapshot context
tasks.description:
Create `test/action-context.test.ts` with integration tests for all context levels. Use the existing test setup pattern (BrowserManager + test server from test/setup.ts or test/test-server.ts). Tests need HTML fixtures that produce DOM changes on click (e.g., a button that adds/removes elements).

Test cases:
1. Delta mode: goto page → snapshot -i → click button that adds element → verify response includes [snapshot-delta] with + line having @ref
2. Full mode: click → verify response includes [snapshot] with complete ARIA tree and valid refs
3. State mode: click → verify response includes [context] line only (no ARIA)
4. No-change action: fill input → verify empty delta (no [snapshot-delta] section)
5. No baseline: first write with delta → verify falls back to full
6. Ref validity: use @ref from delta output in subsequent click → verify it works
7. Backward compat: X-Browse-Context: 1 → state-only behavior

Create a test fixture `test/fixtures/dynamic.html` with a button that adds/removes DOM elements on click.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: click in delta mode produces [snapshot-delta] output with + @eN lines for added elements
Test: fill in delta mode produces no snapshot-delta section (nothing changed in DOM)
Test: refs from delta output resolve to valid elements for subsequent commands

tasks.title: MCP integration tests for snapshot context
tasks.description: Add a test section to `test/mcp.test.ts` verifying context levels work through MCP. Test that write commands return ARIA delta when context level is set to delta. Test that read/meta commands never include context. Test that set context full changes subsequent responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: MCP write command returns ARIA diff when context is delta
Test: MCP browse_snapshot does NOT include context (meta command)
Edge case: MCP context-enriched result works correctly with JSON wrapping
tasks.rollbackPlan: Remove added test section from mcp.test.ts

tasks.title: Update MCP tool descriptions for context levels
tasks.description: Update write command tool descriptions in `src/mcp-tools.ts` to mention that responses can include ARIA snapshot deltas or full snapshots when context level is set. Update `browse_click` description to mention: 'When context is set to delta/full, response includes ARIA snapshot changes with clickable @refs.' Keep descriptions under 300 chars. Do not modify read command descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: browse_click description mentions delta/full context levels and @refs
No read or meta command descriptions are modified
Edge case: all descriptions stay under 300 characters
